excerpt: whoever loves Mawlana Ali in the sense of recognizing his station as khalīfa (successor), who believes in his Imāma (spiritual authority), and obeys him...such a person is liberated from the dominion of the false Imam
source_location_hint: doctrinal commentary on wilāya and Imāma

excerpt: O Ali! Your friend is my friend, and your enemy is my enemy
source_location_hint: declaration of wilāya

excerpt: I have been commanded to fight mankind until they pronounce the Kalima. When they speak this word, their blood and wealth are protected from me, save what Islamic law demands
source_location_hint: section on entry into faith

excerpt: Whosoever recites the Kalima with sincerity enters Paradise...the understanding of its spiritual and physical dimensions and fulfilling the rights they entail
source_location_hint: on the sincere declaration of faith

excerpt: Amīr al-Mu'minīn is first and last in this sense: the beginning of knowledge of the sincere utterance originates in him, and its culminating perfection is likewise bound to his essence
source_location_hint: esoteric exegesis of Quranic verses on Imāma

excerpt: When rational nature itself reveals that every human is both first and last, how much more so the Absolute Man—the Perfect Man—from whose prophethood and executorship all times draw their closure?
source_location_hint: rational demonstration of Imamic supremacy

excerpt: All numbers between are both cause and effect, standing as father and son...one is the first with no first before it, and thousand is the last with no end after it. These two terminals rest in the hands of Ali
source_location_hint: mathematical symbolism of divine order

excerpt: Such was the station of Ali (may Allah have exalted him) in Allah's knowledge: his form appeared first in creation...he is, in this sense as well, first in thought and last in action
source_location_hint: on the precedence of the Imams in divine knowledge

excerpt: True tawḥīd is the purification of the Creator from all physical and spiritual attributes...the outward and inward are attributes of His creatures
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama or Asas al-Taʾwil
source_location_hint: doctrine of God's absolute transcendence

excerpt: The believer is unknown in this world, yet well-known in the highest assembly
source_location_hint: on the hidden status of the righteous

excerpt: Blessed is he who saw me; blessed is he who saw one who saw me; blessed is he who saw one who saw one who saw me
source_location_hint: on spiritual vision of the Prophet and Imams

excerpt: None has known me with right knowledge save Allah and you, O Ali
source_location_hint: declaration of Ali's perfect knowledge

excerpt: This proves that prophet and messenger are manifest in their bodies—seen by every enemy and friend alike—yet hidden in their station and greatness, unknown to any
source_location_hint: on the distinction between manifest body and hidden station

excerpt: Wisdom is the lost treasure of the believer...the difference between manifest and hidden is that his greatness was concealed among them until Allah made it manifest
source_location_hint: esoteric interpretation of Qur'an 93:7

excerpt: I am the First and I am the Last; I am the Hidden and I am the Manifest...the Mighty Eye made this its chapter, and all the forms gathered around it
source_location_hint: comprehensive declaration of Imamic attributes

excerpt: the first human born of God bore the name Ali, and the last who shall appear will also bear the name Ali. Likewise, the names of all eternal portions of the vessels at the beginning of the cycles shall be Ali
source_location_hint: on the eternal recurrence of the Imams

excerpt: all Imams are a part of Ali, and Ali himself is their totality...Ali is the imprint upon all periods and cycles
source_location_hint: on the unity and multiplicity of the Imams

excerpt: The intention of the believer is better than his deed...reward will not be wasted. He will definitely get the reward of his intentions.
source_location_hint: early part of discussion on intention

excerpt: Islam and faith are both founded upon intention and both exist through genuine faith. A modest action performed with pure intention exceeds abundant action devoid of sincere purpose.
source_location_hint: commentary on wilāyat and intention

excerpt: The believer who acquires righteous knowledge discloses it to the world in exemplary character. He learns beneficial sciences from his elders and imparts these to others so that they too may undergo spiritual formation.
source_location_hint: discussion of bee metaphor and knowledge manifestation

excerpt: Whoever prepares a person capable of religious service accomplishes something noble. If he enables that person to undertake scholarly service within the daʿwa al-Hādiyya, Allah shall grant him an exalted station in Paradise—the divine realm which is the Imam's paradise.
source_location_hint: discussion of mosque building hadith and spiritual development

excerpt: In the interpretive sense, men refers to scholars and teachers—the beneficiaries. Women denotes learners or disciples—the benefited. The Imams instruct the Ummah in the sciences of Sharīʿa, taʾwīl, and reality.
source_location_hint: esoteric interpretation of women as a trial

excerpt: The earth symbolizes the Imam of the Age—the center of truth. To anoint oneself is to maintain connection with him. Earth also refers to the proof of Imam al-Zaman. Whoever anoints with the proof develops a spiritual bond and derives from his blessings of knowledge and sanctity.
source_location_hint: discussion of tayammum hadith and Imam connection

excerpt: In the Imam's temple there shall be fewer beneficiaries and more benefactors. Those in the upper limits of daʿwa perform its duties, remaining devoted to teaching and learning.
source_location_hint: commentary on inhabitants of Paradise hadith

excerpt: The Prophet likened his Ahl al-Bayt to Noah's Ark...the Prophet foresaw peril ahead, for otherwise the metaphor would hold no meaning
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis

excerpt: In brief: multitudes were submerged beneath the flood of innovations and doubts. Yet the few who boarded the Prophet's vessel—the Ahl al-Bayt—were preserved from error
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis

excerpt: The essence of all things is ever rare and concentrated. Among humankind, the learned and wise are few whilst the ignorant and debased abound
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis

excerpt: In this vessel of the Ahl al-Bayt, the Ahl al-Bayt related every outward command of the Prophet to its inward reality...Action bereft of knowledge is a body without soul; knowledge devoid of action is a soul without body
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis

excerpt: The Prophet ⟪ar:رسول اﷲ (ﷺ)⟫ declared: 'On the Day of Resurrection, those who fabricate images and idols shall be presented before Allāh'...After him, Mawlānā ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib became the author and guardian through whom that Spirit was transmitted
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis

excerpt: Imām al-Zamān (the Imam of the Age) is the true Name of God...when this seeker questions an apparent scholar and poses even a simple query, that scholar becomes abased and loses all capacity to counter him
source_location_hint: 7th Majlis
